Container.Name: alejandrothegreat
Container.Overview:
Discord music bot with a web dashboard, called Alejandrothegreat. Paste a Spotify link/playlist or just search terms and it plays the matching audio from YouTube (Spotify doesn't allow third-party bots to stream its own audio). Control it with Discord slash commands, `!`-prefix text commands, or by logging into the web dashboard with your Discord account.
Everything - the Discord bot token, Spotify credentials, and an admin password - is configured through a setup wizard at http://[IP]:[PORT:3000]/setup.html after you start the container. No environment variables required.
You'll need a free Discord application (discord.com/developers/applications) and, optionally, a free Spotify application (developer.spotify.com/dashboard) before running the wizard - see the project README for the exact steps.
Container.Description: Discord music bot with a web dashboard. Search Spotify links/playlists (audio streams from YouTube), control playback via slash commands, `!` text commands, or the browser dashboard. Configure the Discord bot token and Spotify credentials through the built-in setup wizard - no environment variables required.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Port for the web dashboard and setup wizard

Config.Name: Data
Config.Description: Stores the SQLite database with your bot credentials, admin password, and per-server settings. Back this up.
